examples.title: Remote Interface Definition
examples.description: Defines the remote interface that all remote objects must implement
examples.explanation: The Remote interface is the foundation of RMI. It must extend java.rmi.Remote, and all methods must declare that they throw RemoteException to handle communication failures.

examples.title: Remote Implementation Class
examples.description: Implements the remote interface and extends UnicastRemoteObject
examples.explanation: The implementation class must implement the remote interface and extend UnicastRemoteObject to be exported as a remote object. The constructor throws RemoteException because it calls the parent constructor that exports the object.

examples.title: RMI Server Program
examples.description: Creates and registers the remote object with RMI registry
examples.explanation: The server creates an instance of the remote implementation and registers it with the RMI registry using Naming.rebind(). The registry runs on port 1099 by default.

examples.title: RMI Client Program
examples.description: Looks up and uses the remote object
examples.explanation: The client uses Naming.lookup() to find the remote object by its URL. It then casts the returned reference to the remote interface type and calls methods as if they were local.

examples.title: RMI Compilation and Execution Steps
examples.description: Commands and process flow for running RMI application
examples.explanation: This shows the complete compilation and execution flow. The rmic tool generates _Stub.class and _Skeleton.class files. The registry must be started before the server runs.
